data.title: Ex-Fed official Bullard backs FOMC's 25-bp cut this week
data.description:
James Bullard, the former St. Louis Fed president and a contender to be the next Federal Reserve Chair, said he wouldn't have supported a 50-basis-point rate cut if he were voting at Wednesday's Federal Open Market Committee.

"I thought the Fed's decision was a good one," he said in an interview on Bloomberg Television. When asked if he would have voted for a 50-bp cut, he said, "No, I don't think so."  Bullard said he expects a "sequence of three moves in a row through the end of the year."

At the FOMC meeting, newly-seated Fed Governor Stephen Miran was the only member to disagree with the central bank's move to lower its benchmark rate by 25 bps to 4.00%-4.25%. Instead, he preferred a 50-bp cut.

A total of 75 bps of easing by year's end would constitute a "significant" move, while leaving the Fed with "optionality" entering 2026, Bullard said. For the dovish members of the FOMC, 50 basis points of cuts in September and October would be "almost as good" as a half percentage point in September, he added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Dream Unlimited renews share buyback program
data.description:
• Dream Unlimited (TSX:DRM:CA) renewed its normal course issuer bid for one year, starting September 23, 2025.
  • The company can repurchase up to 2.32M Class A subordinate voting shares, representing 10% of its public float.
  • The bid will end on September 22, 2026, or earlier if the maximum shares are repurchased.
  • Dream repurchased 264,899 shares under its current bid at an average price of $18.75, totaling ~$5M.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Intel slips as Citi downgrades after Nvidia deal, citing valuation, foundry concerns
data.description:
• Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) was in focus on Friday after Citi downgraded the chipmaker following its deal with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).
  • Shares fell 0.7% in premarket trading.
  • “We expect minimal improvement for Intel as better graphics won't make Intel's CPU better than AMD's given the processor is the main performance driver,” Citi analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients. “In addition, we don't expect much from the AI product given the small [total addressable market] ($1 to $2 billion) and our belief that the stock is pricing in success in its leading-edging foundry business, which we believe has minimal chance to succeed.”
  • Danely lowered his rating on Intel to Sell from Neutral, but upped his price target to $29 from $24.
  • Additionally, with Intel shares up roughly 50% since early August, Danely said investors are likely pricing in a deal for Intel's foundry. “We disagree given our belief that Intel's foundry is years behind [Taiwan Semiconductor] (TSM),” Danely added.

data.title: GBT Technologies to acquire Two Hands Corporation
data.description:
• GBT Technologies (OTCPK:GTCH) has authorized a non-binding indication of interest to acquire all the issued and outstanding shares of Two Hands Corporation (OTCPK:TWOH).
  • The proposed offer contemplates consideration of $0.00625 per share, payable in a to be determined combination of cash and GBT shares, in a future combination of consideration components to be determined in final negotiations between the parties.
  • This proposal includes the assumption by GBT of all outstanding debt obligations of Two Hands.

data.title: Emergent Biosolutions secures $29M in MCM product orders from international government partner
data.description:
• Emergent BioSolutions (NYSE:EBS) on Friday said it has secured new purchase orders from an international government partner for a multi-product supply agreement valued at $29 million.
  • The orders span several product offerings within Emergent’s medical countermeasures business, including countermeasures for smallpox, anthrax and botulism threats, and reflects the increasing priority governments are placing on defense and preparedness.
  • Emergent will receive about $26 million in 2025, which is in addition to the more than $100 million in sales already generated year to date for its MCM portfolio outside the U.S.
  • EBS -1.63% premarket to $7.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ARS Pharma rises after Japan nod for neffy nasal spray for allergy
data.description:
• ARS Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:SPRY) traded higher in the premarket on Friday after the company announced that regulatory authorities in Japan have approved its neffy nasal spray as an emergency treatment for allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis.
  • The approval from Japan’s Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency allows ARS Pharma’s Tokyo-based partner Alfresa (OTCPK:ALFRY) to market the therapy in 1 mg and 2 mg doses for adults and children who weigh at least 15kg.
  • According to a 2020 licensing deal with the company, Alfresa (OTCPK:ARHCF), which expects to launch neffy in Q4 2025, is expected to pay a $2M final regulatory milestone to ARS Pharma (NASDAQ:SPRY) if the drug is listed on the Japanese National Health Institute (NHI) Drug Price List. Under the terms, SPRY is also entitled to sell neffy at a transfer price to Alfresa (ARHCY).

data.title: New York utility regulator endorses controversial NESE pipeline project
data.description:
New York’s Public Service Commission voted to authorize a plan from utility National Grid (NYSE:NGG) to supply natural gas to New York City that includes construction of the controversial Northeast Supply Enhancement pipeline that would run beneath New York Harbor, cbs6albany.com reported late Thursday.

The PSC has no formal role in the pipeline's approval - a decision is imminent from the state Department of Environmental Conservation - but the regulator agreed with National Grid (NYSE:NGG) that steps are needed to address and prevent outages and potential shortfalls in gas supply during peak winter demand; the company maintains that the NESE pipeline is a key part of its strategy.

The regulator told National Grid (NGG) to report on how the utility would optimize natural gas supply sources if the NESE project is or is not placed into service.

The NESE pipeline, proposed by Williams Co.'s (WMB) Transco unit to increase gas supplies from Pennsylvania into New York and New Jersey, has been rejected multiple times in the past by the DEC, citing environmental and public health risks.

Three National Grid (NGG) utilities - Brooklyn Union Gas, KeySpan Gas East and Niagara Mohawk Power - serve 2.5M gas customers in New York.

New York Gov. Hochul has not opposed the project, and her office issued a statement saying she is "pushing an all-of-the-above approach that prioritizes affordability, grid reliability, and economic development."

data.title: PG&E declares $0.025 dividend
data.description:
• PG&E (NYSE:PCG) declares $0.025/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.67%
  • Payable Oct. 15; for shareholders of record Sept. 30; ex-div Sept. 30.
  • See PCG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Fathom prices public offering of 3M shares at $2.00 apiece, stock down over 10%
data.description:
Real estate services platform Fathom Holdings (NASDAQ:FTHM) announced on Friday the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 3M stocks at $2.00 per share.

The company expects to receive net proceeds of ~$5.4M, which are intended to be used for general corporate purposes.

Shares were -10.42% pre-market at $2.15.

The company had announced the proposed primary offering yesterday after market close.

Additionally, the company granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the shares in the offering on the same terms and conditions.

The offering is expected to close on September 22.

data.title: Wayfair unveils performance-driven pay package for CEO
data.description:
Wayfair (NYSE:W) shares are getting a modest boost into Friday’s open after the company announced a new compensation package for CEO Niraj Shah that includes incentives for the company’s performance.

According to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Shah will now receive 5M performance stock units (PSU) split in 6 tranches over a decade if the company achieves certain goals. These include stock price appreciation to $176 to as much as $679 to receive the final tranche. This assumes a premium in the company’s share price of 100% to 675% over the period and $75B in incremental market value at the final tranche.

The award also includes a one-year post-vesting holding period for tranches two through six and requires Shah to remain as CEO through each vesting tranche. The plan becomes effective on September 19.  

“The CEO award is designed to retain and incentivize Mr. Shah in light of his consistent and unmatched leadership since co-founding the company,” the filing states.

The compensation plan requires the approval of the company’s shareholders.

Since the beginning of the year, Wayfair (NYSE:W) shares have nearly doubled in value compared to a 13% increase in the S&P 500.
